backend/main.mo: Empty Motoko actor serving as the backend entry point with no implemented functionality.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting that the caffeine.ai attribution/publish metadata was removed from the app layout/footer, with high confidence, referencing Layout.tsx.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON-formatted summaries of all frontend source files describing each component, page, utility, and data file in the project.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind CSS entry file defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark theme color tokens including backgrounds, borders, charts, and sidebar colors.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point titled 'The Daddy TGM' that mounts the React app into a root div and loads the main TypeScript module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Configures TanStack Router with six routes (Home, Reader, Archive, Soapbox, About, Classifieds) all wrapped in the Layout component.
frontend/src/components/ClassifiedAdCard: Renders a single classified ad card with retro newsprint styling, showing headline, body, contact, and a special badge with username/timestamp for user-submitted ads.
Renders classified ads grouped by category in a responsive grid, showing user-submitted ads before static ads within each category section with decorative headers.
frontend/src/components/ClassifiedSubmis: Form component allowing users to submit their own classified ads with category, headline, body, and contact fields, persisting via classifiedsUtils and showing a confirmation message on success.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Full-page layout wrapper composing Masthead, Navigation, diagonal stripe dividers, main content area, and a footer with copyright notice (caffeine.ai attribution removed).
frontend/src/components/Masthead.tsx: Magazine masthead component with retro serif typography, flicker animation, diagonal stripe accents, and decorative repeating-gradient borders for an early 2000s athletic aesthetic.
frontend/src/components/Navigation.tsx: Renders the site navigation bar with links to all main pages, supporting both desktop and mobile (hamburger menu) layouts with active link highlighting.
frontend/src/components/PageRenderer.tsx: Renders different types of magazine page content (movie posters, comic strips, product ads, reviews, letters, back-page items) based on the page type passed as a prop.
frontend/src/data/classifiedsLibrary.ts: Defines TypeScript types and a static library of humorous retro-style classified ads across categories like For Sale, Personals, Help Wanted, and more.
frontend/src/data/contentLibrary.ts: Defines TypeScript interfaces and static data arrays for all satirical magazine content types including spoof movie posters, comic strips, fake ads, reviews, and letters to the editor.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom React hook that creates and returns an ICP backend actor (authenticated or anonymous) using the current Internet Identity, invalidating dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an Internet Identity authentication context and hook, managing login/logout state, AuthClient creation, and identity persistence via a React context provider.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that imports retro/athletic Google Fonts, sets up Tailwind layers, defines a 1970s earth-tone CSS custom property palette, and declares reusable component classes like retro-masthead, athletic-heading, jersey-number, and diagonal-stripe.
frontend/src/main.tsx: React app entry point that mounts the app with TanStack Query, an InternetIdentity auth provider, and the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/AboutPage.tsx: Static About page displaying fictional staff bios and a humorous founding story for the satirical magazine 'The Daddy TGM', styled with retro CSS classes.
frontend/src/pages/ArchivePage.tsx: Archive page that loads and displays all past magazine issues from local storage, allowing navigation to any issue in the reader view.
frontend/src/pages/ClassifiedsPage.tsx: Classifieds page that renders a styled masthead, a user ad submission form, and a grid of both static library ads and user-submitted classified ads.
frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx: Home page that generates today's magazine issue using a date-based seed, displays the cover page with a jersey-number watermark, and shows teaser cards for the first few inside pages.
frontend/src/pages/ReaderPage.tsx: Renders a paginated 50-page magazine reader, generating pages from a seeded issue, with navigation controls and a header showing issue number and date.
frontend/src/pages/SoapboxPage.tsx: Implements a retro-styled community message board where users can post, like, and reply to messages, with data persisted via localStorage.
frontend/src/utils/classifiedsUtils.ts: Provides CRUD utilities for user-submitted classified ads stored in localStorage, including submission with auto-generated usernames and category tagging.
frontend/src/utils/issueGenerator.ts: Generates a deterministic 50-page issue layout by shuffling a content pool with a seeded RNG, always placing a back-page item on page 50.
frontend/src/utils/issueUtils.ts: Provides helpers for computing issue numbers and seeds from dates, archiving viewed issues to localStorage, and formatting date strings for display.
frontend/src/utils/prng.ts: Implements a seedable mulberry32 pseudo-random number generator with shuffle, pick, pickN, and pickIndex utilities for deterministic content selection.
frontend/src/utils/soapboxUtils.ts: Manages soapbox post and reply data in localStorage, including adding posts/replies, liking posts, generating fake 70s-style usernames, and formatting relative timestamps.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Configures Tailwind CSS with a 1970s retro and early-2000s athletic color palette, custom font families, and plugins for typography, container queries, and animations.
project_state.json: Tracks the full project state (version 9) for 'The Daddy TGM' retro magazine app, including architecture notes, all implemented features, known issues, and content pool details. Serves as the source of truth for build planning and feature tracking.
